zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Moray of Bothwell於1220年出生在英國蘇格蘭時，她的父親William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray25歲，母親Elizabeth MacDuff25歲。她於1235年與John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham在蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1250年在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾伊斯威特享年30歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Moray of Bothwell于1220年出生在英国苏格兰时，她的父亲William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray25岁，母亲Elizabeth MacDuff25岁。她于1235年与John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham在苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1250年在英国苏格兰雷福福爾伊斯威特享年30岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrey
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Morey  .

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moree
meanings: French (Morée): habitational name from a place so named in Loir-et-Cher.

variants.name: Morie
meanings: Altered form of Moree, itself perhaps an altered form of English or French Morey (compare 2 below) or of Irish Morrow .
English (London): variant of   Morey  . This surname is rare in Britain.
French: unexplained. This surname is rare in France.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Bothwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire) and northern Irish: habitational name from a place in Lanarkshire named Bothwell, from Middle English both(e) ‘bothy, small hut’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Moray of Bothwell was born in 1220, in Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth MacDuff, was 25. She married John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham in 1235, in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1250, in Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 30.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Moray of Bothwell est née en 1220, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth MacDuff, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham en 1235, à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1250, à Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 30 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Moray of Bothwell 1220 in Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray 25 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth MacDuff 25. Sie hat 1235 in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1250 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Moray of Bothwell è nata il nel 1220 nel Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth MacDuff, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham nel 1235, nel Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1250, nel Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 30 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Moray of Bothwellが1220年にイギリススコットランドで生まれた時，父William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Morayは25歳，母Elizabeth MacDuffは25歳であった。1235年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムでJohn Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eagleshamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1250年にイギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアストウッドで30歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Moray of Bothwell(이)가 1220년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth MacDuff(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1235년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에서 John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1250년 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 이스트우드에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Moray of Bothwell nasceu em 1220, em Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth MacDuff, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham em 1235, em Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1250, em Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 30 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Moray of Bothwell родилась в 1220 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth MacDuff, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham в 1235 г. в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1250 г. в местности эаствуд, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 30 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Moray of Bothwell nació en 1220, en Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Moray of Bothwell, Canon of Moray, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elizabeth MacDuff, tenía 25 años. Se casó con John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham en 1235, en Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1250, en Eastwood, Renfrewshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 30 años.
